SnowDistribution: Firm snow. Good visibility. Winds approx 10 - 20 mph fresh, cooling.
Comment: The snowpack will be firm and icy. Any slip or fall could lead to a prolonged slide.
WeatherInfluences: A dry and bright period is expected with the freezing level dropping to 450 metres overnight, then rising to 750 metres during the day. Winds will be Northerly light to moderate through Friday night, backing South during the daytime while steadily increasing to become fresh.
SnowStability: No significant change is expected. The snowpack will be firm, icy and generally stable all in locations  Coverage is good on South-West through North-West to North-East aspects above 700 metres and is extensive on the White Mounth. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A predominantly dry day with the freezing level around 900 metres. Moderate to light North-Westerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is generally well consolidated and has good stability throughout. The snow surface is firm, softening slightly during the day below 800 metres and where exposed to direct sun. Despite the recent thaw, coverage remains good on South-West through North-West to North-East aspects above 700 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Patchy cover of firm snow.  Good visibility on the summits most of the day.  Winds 10-20mph.
Comment: Snow will be very firm with potential for serious consequences in the event of a trip or slip.
WeatherInfluences: A ridge of high pressure will bring mostly dry, cold conditions with moderate South-Westerly winds. A few light showers possible overnight falling as snow above around 500 metres. The freezing level will be around 500 metres rising slightly thorugh the day.
SnowStability: The existing snowpack will be well bonded, very firm and stable. Any new snow deposits are not expected to be significant. The snow cover is patchy in places however significant areas will remain in some corries, gullies and around some summits. These areas will be very firm and mostly exist on North through East to South-East aspects above around 750 metres. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A mainly dry day with moderate North-Westerly winds. The freezing level fell to around 700 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is well bonded and stable. Snow cover is patchy in places with some significant areas remaining in corries, gullies and around summits above around 750 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Diminishing snowpack, good above 800 metres.  Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells. Breezy on the tops
Comment: A cold clear day and a refreezing snowpack should provide a bright day and good conditions in the mountains. Snowpack will be firm and icy with serious runout potential.
WeatherInfluences: Cold and clear conditions expected over the forecast period with summit  temperatures of minus 5 deg C expected overnight, a few degrees warmer during the afternoon. Winds generally light South-Westerly. A dry bright day is expected.
SnowStability: The diminished snowpack will continue to consolidate  and refreeze on all aspects and levels and will be stable. Greatest amounts are mostly above 950m on North-West through North to Easterly aspects. Patchy and snow ribbons below.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A cooler day with occasional showers falling as light sleet and snow at summit levels for a time, generally clearing by midday. Winds becoming light during the afternoon. Freezing level around 1000m.
ObservedSnowStability: The diminished snowpack has consolidated in the cooler conditions, a slight surface crust has developed but the snowpack, although soft and moist to depth at all altitudes, is generally stable throughout.  Greatest amounts lie above 950 metres on North-West through North to Easterly aspects. Ribbons and patches of snow below this level but above 800m.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Most continuous snow cover is above 800 metres which is firm often icy at higher elevations. Reasonable visibility with light Westerly winds 10-15 mph.
Comment: The snowpack surface is likely to be firm and icy with serious consequences in the likelihood of a slip or a slide.
WeatherInfluences: The freezing level will be around 700 metres, it is expected to be dry and clear, maybe even sunny. Winds will be light Southerly at first, strengthening through the day becoming moderate or strong.
SnowStability: Colder conditions will maintain a consolidated snowpack. During the morning the surface layer will be frozen, firm and icy on all aspects some softening may take place after midday. Deepest snow amounts will exist on North-West through North to Easterly aspects above 850 metres other aspects will have lesser amounts. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Colder conditions last night affected the area with some light new snow falling mainly above 850 metres. It was a dry morning with just the odd light isolated shower in the afternoon. The freezing level fell to around 900 metres, winds were light to moderate from a Westerly direction.
ObservedSnowStability: The overnight cooler conditions have stabilised the moist snowpack of recent days. The dusting of new snow that arrived is not deep or extensive and exists mainly above 850 metres. The snowpack is generally stable on all aspects and is often firm and icy above 900 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Firm snow or breakable crust. Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells. Winds 10 - 20 mph.
Comment: Snowpack surface becoming very firm. Deteriorating weather for Saturday night and Sunday.
WeatherInfluences: It will remain dry with moderate, variable winds which increase from the South in the afternoon. The freezing level will be below the snowline.
SnowStability: The older snowpack will remain firm and stable. Isolated and shallow areas of windslab will be present, mainly on North to East aspects above 1100 metres with features such as gully tops and corrie rims most affected. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: It was a mainly dry day with fresh West-South-Westerly winds easing and veering in the afternoon. The freezing level was around 900 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The older snowpack is generally stable, with a firm surface above 900 metres. Isolated and shallow areas of moderately bonded windslab are present, mainly on North to East aspects above 1100 metres with features such as gully tops and corrie rims most affected. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Snowpack firming up above 900 metres, breakable crust in some areas especially below this altitude.  Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells. Winds approx 10 to 20 mph.
Comment: The snowpack is expected to firm up with serious run out potential on steep slopes.
WeatherInfluences: Light Westerly winds will increase to moderate through the afternoon and back to become Southerly in direction.  Apart from a trace of new snow in the early evening, it is expected to be dry throughout with clear skies. The 600 metre freezing level will rise to 800 metres during the day.
SnowStability: The snowpack will continue to consolidate and become firm well bonded and stable throughout. The greatest extent of snow will be above 800 metres in areas such as coire back walls, flanks, and burn lines. Large cornices will continue to overhang North-West to Northerly aspects.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Mainly dry with occasional light snow showers on moderate Westerly winds. The freezing level steadily lowered to around 1000 metres by midday.
ObservedSnowStability: There is a dusting of new snow on all aspects above 900 metres. The old snowpack continues to consolidate and has developed a firm crust above 950 metres, stability is generally improving. Large unstable cornices overhang North-West to Northerly aspects. The avalanche hazard is Low.
